incorrects.description: Student built a rectangle with area 30 but dimensions other than 5×6

params.description: Fixed side length of 5 pulses to draw attention.

steps.text: You have the right area. But one side must be 5. Look at the locked side. How many rows do you need to make 30?

incorrects.description: Student built a rectangle with incorrect area on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. The area needs to be 30. Count the squares in your rectangle.

incorrects.description: Student built a rectangle with incorrect area on second attempt

steps.text: Let's think about this together. One side is 5. To get an area of 30, how many groups of 5 do you need? Try adding or removing rows.

incorrects.description: Student built an incorrect rectangle three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: Grid animates to show 5×6 rectangle building row by row. Labels reveal: W=5, H=6, area=30.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. One side is locked at 5. To get area 30, I need 6 rows because 5 times 6 equals 30. The rectangle is 5 wide and 6 tall. That's how we build a rectangle with side length 5 and area 30.

incorrects.description: Student reversed the factors: wrote 6 × 5 instead of 5 × 6

steps.text: You have the right numbers, but check the order. The width is 5. Which number should go first in the multiplication?

incorrects.description: Student filled sentence incorrectly on first attempt

steps.text: Look at the labels on the rectangle. Which number is the area? Which are the side lengths?

incorrects.description: Student filled sentence incorrectly on second attempt

steps.text: Here's a hint: the area is 30. That goes first and last. The width and height multiply to make it. Read the labels and fill them in order.

incorrects.description: Student filled sentence incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Blanks fill one by one: 30 is a multiple of 5 because 5 × 6 = 30.

steps.text: Let me walk you through this. The area is 30, so that goes in the first blank and the last blank. 30 is a multiple of 5, so 5 goes in the second blank. The rectangle is 5 wide and 6 tall, so 5 times 6 goes in the middle. The sentence is: 30 is a multiple of 5 because 5 times 6 equals 30.
